title: Vegetable oil supply per person vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: Average vegetable oil supply per capita, measured in kilograms per year versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Vegetable oil supply per person vs. GDP per capita

Average vegetable oil supply per capita, measured in kilograms per year versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot displays the relationship between vegetable oil supply per person and GDP per capita for various countries in 2023. The vertical axis measures vegetable oil supply in kilograms per year per capita, ranging from 0 to 35 kg, while the horizontal axis represents GDP per capita in international dollars at 2021 prices, plotted on a logarithmic scale from $2,000 to $100,000. Data points are color-coded by continent: North America (orange), South America (brown), Africa (purple), Europe (blue), Asia (teal), and Oceania (light blue). Countries with lower GDP per capita, such as DR Congo, Niger, and Burkina Faso, cluster in the bottom-left area with vegetable oil supplies generally below 10 kg. As GDP increases, vegetable oil supply tends to rise, though with significant variation. For instance, Guinea-Bissau shows a supply near 20 kg despite a GDP under $5,000. In the mid-range of GDP, countries like Brazil and Algeria reach supplies around 25 kg. High-income nations display diverse patterns: Canada and the United States sit near the top right with supplies exceeding 25 kg and GDPs over $50,000. Conversely, some wealthy European nations like Denmark and Norway appear in the lower-right quadrant, with GDPs exceeding $50,000 but vegetable oil supplies below 10 kg. Australia and Ireland occupy the upper-right section with high GDP and supplies between 15 and 25 kg. The distribution indicates that while higher income often correlates with higher oil supply, specific national levels vary widely across all income groups.
